Question: Provide a brief company overview, including employee count, office footprint, and where customer data is hosted.
Vendor Response: Acme Analytics, Inc. is a B2B SaaS company providing analytics dashboards for mid-market finance teams. The company has 42 employees, operates fully remote with no physical offices, and hosts customer data on AWS (us-east-1 primary, us-west-2 for disaster recovery). Production and staging environments run in separate AWS accounts. (Company Profile)

Question: List all security certifications and attestations currently held (e.g., SOC 2, ISO 27001), including report dates and any noted exceptions.
Vendor Response: Acme holds a SOC 2 Type II report issued March 2026, covering January through December 2025, with no exceptions noted. ISO 27001 is not held. (SOC 2 Type II Report, 2026-03)

Question: Describe encryption of customer data at rest and in transit, including key management and rotation. Do you support customer-managed keys (CMK)?
Vendor Response: Customer data at rest is encrypted with AES-256 using AWS KMS, with keys rotated annually via automatic rotation (Encryption Policy v2.3 §4, §4.2). Data in transit is protected with TLS 1.2 minimum on all public endpoints; TLS 1.3 is preferred where supported (Encryption Policy v2.3 §5). Customer-managed keys (CMK) are not addressed in our current documentation — NEEDS OWNER INPUT: confirm whether CMK is supported, and if so, describe the mechanism.

Question: Is single sign-on with multi-factor authentication enforced for all employees and contractors? Name your identity provider.
Vendor Response: Yes — SSO via Okta with MFA is enforced for all employees and contractors. (Access Control Policy v1.8 §2)

Question: Describe your access review cadence and the timeline for revoking access upon employee termination.
Vendor Response: Access reviews are conducted quarterly. Upon employee termination, access is revoked within 24 hours (Access Control Policy v1.8 §5). Role-based access follows least-privilege principles, and production access is limited to on-call engineers via short-lived credentials (Access Control Policy v1.8 §3).

Question: Describe your penetration testing program: frequency, most recent test date, performing firm, and remediation of critical/high findings.
Vendor Response: Penetration testing is performed annually. The most recent test was conducted in November 2025 by Redwood Security. No critical findings were identified; two high-severity findings were remediated within 30 days. (Pen Test Executive Summary, 2025-11)

Question: What is your log retention period? Can retention be extended beyond 12 months upon customer request?
Vendor Response: Log retention is set at 12 months. AWS CloudTrail and application logs are shipped to Datadog with alerting configured for authentication anomalies and privilege changes (Logging & Monitoring Standard v1.2). Whether retention can be extended beyond 12 months per customer request is not addressed in our current documentation — NEEDS OWNER INPUT: confirm whether extended log retention is supported and the mechanism for enabling it.

Question: Do you maintain a documented incident response plan? What is your customer notification commitment following a confirmed breach?
Vendor Response: Yes, a documented incident response plan is maintained with a severity matrix and on-call rotation (Incident Response Plan v2.0). Affected customers are notified within 72 hours of a confirmed breach involving their data (Incident Response Plan v2.0 §6).

Question: Describe your backup strategy and RTO/RPO targets. Provide the date and results of your most recent disaster recovery test.
Vendor Response: Daily encrypted backups are performed with cross-region replication to us-west-2. Target RTO is 8 hours and target RPO is 24 hours (BC/DR Summary v1.1). The date and results of the most recent disaster recovery test are not documented in our current corpus — NEEDS OWNER INPUT: provide the most recent DR test date and pass/fail results.

Question: Do you perform background checks on new hires? Identify the screening vendor and the scope of checks performed.
Vendor Response: Background checks are performed on all new hires (Employee Handbook §7). The screening vendor and scope of checks performed are not documented in our current corpus — NEEDS OWNER INPUT: provide the background-check vendor name and the scope of checks (criminal, credit, etc.).

Question: Describe endpoint protections on employee devices (MDM, disk encryption, EDR).
Vendor Response: Endpoint protections include Microsoft Intune MDM on all devices, full-disk encryption (BitLocker on Windows, FileVault on macOS), and Microsoft Defender for Endpoint (EDR) deployed fleet-wide. (Endpoint Standard v1.0)

Question: Provide a current list of subprocessors with access to customer data. Is a Data Processing Agreement available?
Vendor Response: Current subprocessors with access to customer data: AWS (hosting), Datadog (logging/monitoring), Stripe (billing), Google Workspace (email/docs), and Zendesk (support) (Subprocessor List, 2026-01). A Data Processing Agreement is available on request and incorporates GDPR Standard Contractual Clauses (Privacy Summary).

Question: Have you appointed a Data Protection Officer? Describe your GDPR and CCPA compliance measures.
Vendor Response: Acme does not have a dedicated Data Protection Officer appointed. A DPA is available on request and incorporates GDPR Standard Contractual Clauses. CCPA requests can be submitted to privacy@acme.example, with the privacy policy available at acme.example/privacy. (Privacy Summary)
